item.title:
RTEgaa: RTÉ GAA @RTEgaa · 1min "I didn't feel like something significant was missing"
Brian Fenton opens up on life after footb
item.description:
RTÉ GAA @RTEgaa · 1min "I didn't feel like something significant was missing"
Brian Fenton opens up on life after football as he settles into his first appearance on the RTÉ GAA Podcast
#RTEGAA Podcast: https:// rte.ie/sport/football /2026/0415/1568366-fenton-in-the-big-chair-as-dubs-enter-the-football-fray/ … 1:33 34

item.description:

item.title: Score_Beo: Score Beo @Score_Beo · 11min Provincial championships are often dominated by the traditional powers, but every so often
item.description:
Score Beo @Score_Beo · 11min Provincial championships are often dominated by the traditional powers, but every so often an underdog breaks through and twists the story of a summer.
These are some of the most memorable shock provincial wins in Gaelic football.
()
@AllianzIreland 1 1 178

item.title:
Score_Beo: Score Beo @Score_Beo · 11min En respuesta a @Score_Beo Clare (1992)
Clare’s 1992 campaign stands as one of the great b
item.description:
Score Beo @Score_Beo · 11min En respuesta a @Score_Beo Clare (1992)
Clare’s 1992 campaign stands as one of the great breakthrough stories in Munster football, a summer when they shook off expectation and made history by winning the provincial title, their first in seventy-five years.
With John Maughan at the helm, Clare brought a Mostrar más 1 146

item.title:
Score_Beo: Score Beo @Score_Beo · 11min En respuesta a @Score_Beo Westmeath (2004)
Westmeath’s 2004 Leinster Championship triumph
item.description:
Score Beo @Score_Beo · 11min En respuesta a @Score_Beo Westmeath (2004)
Westmeath’s 2004 Leinster Championship triumph remains one of the great provincial shocks of the modern era.
Under the management of Páidí Ó Sé, their run gathered real momentum as they saw off Offaly and Meath before setting up a final clash with the reigning Mostrar más 130
